overview.short: Itahara Uparwar is a village in Deegh Mandal, Bhadohi district, Uttar Pradesh State.
overview.description: Itahara Uparwar is a village in Deegh Mandal, Bhadohi district, Uttar Pradesh State. Itahara Uparwar is 37.9 km from its District Main City Bhadohi and 221 km from its State Main City Lucknow. At this place the Ganges flows to the west to east. A temple, Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham, is situated in this village.

topPlaces.name: Konia Kshetra
topPlaces.description: Konia Kshetra is a region of Bhadohi district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The Ganges River flows through its west end and changes its direction at Checuwan - Bhurra village, where the river's turbulence is reduced.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Prayagraj Airport (Bamrauli) (56 km) and the nearest railway station is Prayagraj Junction (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham?
faq.a: Stays in Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Baba Gangeshwarnath Dham?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sherpur Uparwar, Bahupura Uparwar, Duguna Uparwar, Konia Kshetra, Deeghuparwar.
